title: Fanservice in Evangelion Good or Bad
display_title: Fanservice in Evangelion Good or Bad
wikitext:
[]: Fanservice in Evangelion leads to odd juxtapositions             Quote Post by QuantumPete » Mon Jul 11, 2005 6:24 pm Just a quick note on something I noticed while watching this series for the first time recently: The "next episode" summaries at the end of each episode can sometimes be inadvertently hilarious, due to the combination of hard core philosophical questions with certain other elements... As in: "Next time, Shinji must face the meaning of existence and decide which is worse: a life of isolated self-loathing in which one neglects one's duty as a member of society, or a life piloting a murderous beast in order to stop god's minions from bringing about a near-certain apocalypse. Plus, PLENTY OF FANSERVICE FOR YA! Looky dem boobies!" :?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Mon Jul 11, 2005 6:38 pm I have notice that she makes it sound rather like an old-time cinema serial, especially in the dub! "Next Evangelion: As the dreary rain pours down, Shinji, overwhelmed and unable to overcome his weaknesses tries to run away from Misato! It's acceptable, however, considering that he is only fourteen..." "The Organisation brings back the aimlessly wandering Shinji. There no kind words waiting to ease his feelings - except from the two boys." "It's the next episode, Rain, Escape and Afterwards . Of course, I'll give you lots of fan service, too!"[/b]

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 11, 2005 6:50 pm You know i've never really watched these, but if their full of fan service i'll have to look into it. Misato so pretty :)

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Jul 11, 2005 7:00 pm well, when they were airing, i'm sure it was a hook to get in people who didnt really want to see some teenager being depressed

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Jul 12, 2005 11:15 pm sadsadshinji wrote: well, when they were airing, i'm sure it was a hook to get in people who didnt really want to see some teenager being depressed

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 12, 2005 11:33 pm Not one unattractive woman, eh? Do you see something in Naoko that Gendo didn't? (Since, as we all know, he vigorously scrubbed his body for two hours every time she jumped his bone.)

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 13, 2005 2:07 pm Reichu wrote: Not one unattractive woman, eh? Do you see something in Naoko that Gendo didn't? (Since, as we all know, he vigorously scrubbed his body for two hours every time she jumped his bone.)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Jul 13, 2005 2:16 pm Reichu wrote: Not one unattractive woman, eh? Do you see something in Naoko that Gendo didn't? (Since, as we all know, he vigorously scrubbed his body for two hours every time she jumped his bone.)

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 13, 2005 2:21 pm well.. after all, it is anime... so unless they made her like one of the doll girls in EoE (sandbox scene), she almost couldnt possibly look ugly

[]: Fanservice in Evangelion: Good or Bad?             Quote Post by 0x2eleven » Sun May 30, 2010 3:07 am Throughout the entire Evangelion franchise (and many anime franchises for that matter) there is "fanservice," or gratuitous sexual imagery and innuendo, typically focused on the female characters. At times, particularly in Rebuild so far, I've found the inclusion of most of this stuff to be pretty tasteless and potentially damaging to the image of what is truly a masterful work of art. I would like for as many people as humanly possible to be able to experience Evangelion, but I fear most people unaccustomed to anime norms wouldn't want to sit through the continuous hyper low-angle boob and ass shots of girls in their early teens, and might even interpret it as sexism and depraved perversion. I get that it's supposed to be funny/sexy, but I, personally, usually only find it to be extremely clever (when Asuka first meets Pen Pen in 2.0), mildly distracting, and unsuitable for such an otherwise distinguished work. Ultimately, I think Evangelion would have a better chance at gaining the mainstream recognition and acceptance it deserves outside of Japan if there was little-to-no fanservice. How do you feel about the fanservice in Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun May 30, 2010 3:31 am There've been other threads on this topic (you should make use of our search function and thread list up there in the menubar), the last of which turned bad fast and got locked. I'm willing to let this one go for now but it's going to be kept on a short leash. Let's not let it degrade into what the other one did.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 30, 2010 3:41 am Having come to NGE after starting with approximately contemporary titles like Dirty Pair Flash and BurnUp Excess I kept on waiting for the promised Saabisu, Saabisu! to arrive, and meanwhile noting that they cleverly composed shots to distract us all from the fact that they were cutting costs by not animating lip-flaps. Compared with currently airing shows like Seikon no Qwaser and Ichiban Ushiro no Dai Maou , NGE is essentially innocent.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun May 30, 2010 9:31 am There’s surprisingly little fanservice in NGE to begin with—there’s a bit more in rebuild, but only a few scenes that are all that jarring, and even they’re not that bad compared to most series out there. 0x2eleven wrote: I would like for as many people as humanly possible to be able to experience Evangelion, but I fear most people unaccustomed to anime norms wouldn't want to sit through the continuous hyper low-angle boob and ass shots of girls in their early teens, and might even interpret it as sexism and depraved perversion.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Sun May 30, 2010 9:40 am So long as it's funny I honestly don't care for fan service so long as it doesn't ruin fight scenes; amazingly, that rarely happens.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun May 30, 2010 10:10 am Mr. Tines wrote: ...NGE is essentially innocent.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun May 30, 2010 10:37 am most examples of fanservice in the series and movie I took as either subverting such impulses with massive awkwardness or illustrating Shinji's wandering eyes. Just be thankful we didn't get a misty crotch-cam of Kaworu in the bath. 2.22 annoys me because it has instances that seem to serve no purpose other than to invite the audience to perv out, and often at times when there's nobody else around whose gaze we could be following. that said, my current avatar would be an example that 2.22 handled well.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun May 30, 2010 10:37 am 0x2eleven wrote: How do you feel about the fanservice in Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Sun May 30, 2010 1:54 pm NemZ wrote: there's nobody else around whose gaze we could be following.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 30, 2010 2:18 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Having come to NGE after starting with approximately contemporary titles like Dirty Pair Flash and BurnUp Excess ...

[]:             Quote Post by 0x2eleven » Sun May 30, 2010 2:34 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: In the case of Evangelion, removing nudity and sexuality from the work with obviously reduce both the impact and the meaning of the work in several ways.
